A novel multimodal framework for automatic
recognition of individual cattle based on hybrid
features using sparse stacked denoising autoencoder
and group sparse representation techniques

Santosh Kumar1           · Sunil Kumar2 · Mehak Shaﬁ3 · Mithilesh Kumar Chaube4

Received: 12 December 2020 / Revised: 5 March 2021 / Accepted: 21 February 2022 /
Published online: 8 April 2022
© The Author(s), under exclusive licence to Springer Science+Business Media, LLC, part of Springer Nature 2022

Abstract
Recently, visual animal bio-metrics have attracted much attention for identifying endan-
gered species and animals based on their prominent bio-metric features. This study explores
a new possibility of using facial recognition for an inexpensive and user-friendly bio-metric
modality to identify cattle. This paper proposes an automatic recognition system to identify
cattle based on face and muzzle image features using sparse Stacked Denoising Autoen-
coder (SDAE) and group sparse representation techniques. The discriminatory features are

ntify
cattle based on face and muzzle image features using sparse Stacked Denoising Autoen-
coder (SDAE) and group sparse representation techniques. The discriminatory features are
extracted from cattle’s facial image and muzzle images using sparse SDAE and Deep Belief
Networks (DBN) methods to represent extracted features. It mitigates the need for individ-
ual bio-metric feature representation from the single face and muzzle bio-metric modality
features at various score levels. The single modality features and multi-features are fused at
score level to represent better and classify individual cattle using group sparse representation
technique. The proposed system’s performance is compared with holistic and handcrafted
texture feature extraction and representation technique and current state of the art methods.
Author shows that the proposed recognition system yield 96.85% accuracy for identifying
individual cattle based on the multi-feature-based representation of cattle’s face and muzzle
features.

    Cattle identification is necessary for registration, enrollment of bio-metric identity,
tracking and monitoring of individual cattle in traditional livestock management frame-
works. Cattle identification also provides the solution for vaccination facility, tracking, and
registration of individual animal [12, 22, 25, 26].
    The traditional cattle identification frameworks include ear-tattooing, the embedding of
microchips, ear-tagging based system, freeze marking methods, hot iron, and leg marking-
based techniques. It also includes artificial marking methods such as electric signal based
identification method and Radio Frequency Identification (e.g., RFID) for identification and
tracking of individual animal [4].

   In the literature, there is no availability of computer vision, visual animal bio-metrics
based tracking and identification framework for the registration, tracking, and monitoring
of cattle using face and minutiae features. The traditional cattle identification includes only
invasive methods for identification and it also provided low levels of accuracy [25].
    To solve these problems, several interdisciplinary researchers proposed semi-automatic
cattle recognition system based on muzzle print images using image processing and machine
learning techniques. The first muzzle print-based recognition system has been proposed for
the identification of individual cattle by Minagawa et al. [32]. The proposed system was
trained on captured 170 muzzle print images from different races.

cess which includes several pre-processing steps. These steps are used to remove noises,
resize, and crop the captured print image database of cattle manually [10]. Therefore, recog-

process. It needs huge human resources and staff members to keep holding cattle’s heads
with strong ropes/halts to capture the print images of cattle’s nose. Hence, it is an expensive
and time-consuming process for the acquisition of nose images of cattle.
    Moreover, there is no availability of the standard printed nose image of cattle databases
in the public domain to provide a better learning platform for various researchers, veterinary
scientists, and new practitioners in interdisciplinary research. It also poses a significant

    Several researchers reported that cattle’s muzzle (nose) consists of unique bio-metric
traits such as ridges, beads, granule, and vibrissae. The topological structures of beads and
the ridges’ bio-metric patterns are irregular, which looks like islands. The ridge looks similar
to rivers like structure and separates the core bead features. It plays a vital role in the unique
identification of cattle.
    In similar direction, Awad and Hassaballah [34] proposed a classification system using
the bag-of-visual-words technique for extracting holistic features from the muzzle print
images for cattle identification.
    In which they reported that it was difficult to restrict body movement variations of cattle,
implying that holistic feature extraction algorithms have not provided good results. The arti-

   The bio-metric features of face and muzzle features of cattle are a unique bio-metric
identifiers. The minutiae consist of ridge bifurcation and ridge termination. The ridge bifur-
cation, and ridge termination or endings are important bio-metric features in the muzzle
images. The topological structure of the minutiae features is an immutable and unique
bio-metric identifier for cattle, and it is also invariant with aging and any deformations [6].

1.1 Motivation

Computer vision based animal recognition included different techniques such as holistic
features extractors, handcrafted local feature descriptor methods for classification of animal
based on their bio-metric features and phenotype appearances. Among different subspace
analysis based holistic feature representation methods, the unsupervised learning technique
includes statistical-based learning methods such as Eigen-faces or Eigen vectors for better
feature representation.
    The supervised machine learning techniques include Linear Discriminant Analysis
(LDA), Kernel-LDA, and Fisher discriminant analysis, Fisherfaces methods and Locality
Preserving Projections (LPP) optimally preserves the neighborhood structure of extraction
of features from the bio-metric image data sets.
    These techniques are the most illustrative ones, based on a selection of discriminatory
features for a better representation in the feature subspace [4, 12, 13, 22, 23, 25, 26, 39, 49].

techniques are the most illustrative ones, based on a selection of discriminatory
features for a better representation in the feature subspace [4, 12, 13, 22, 23, 25, 26, 39, 49].
These are achieved by using the Principal Component Analysis (PCA) and LDA [39], and
other holistic feature extraction and representation techniques respectively. However, PCA
and LDA method estimate only the global feature scatter of training samples, and they fail
to reveal the essential data structures non-linearly embedded in high dimensional space. For
mitigating these significant limitations, manifold learning techniques are proposed [8].
    Keeping in view that all the data rest on lower dimensional manifold of the high
dimensional space [26]. The different representation learning approaches include locality
preserving projection, unsupervised discriminant projection are proposed in the filed of
visual animal bio-metrics [4, 12, 13, 22, 23, 25, 26, 39, 49]. The success of manifold learn-
ing techniques imply that the high dimensional based images are represented sparsely by
selecting discriminatory features based on described or coded by the representative samples
on the manifold [8].

1.   A novel automatic framework is proposed for recognition of cattle based hybrid fea-
     tures from the face and muzzle image of cattle using using group sparse representation
     technique, Spare Stacked Denoising Autoencoder (SSDAE) methods and Deep Belief

works of an autoencoder is to “encode” an input (X) by mapping it to the hidden layers
of the proposed system or model in a deterministic manner. The encoder is consist of a
two-layered structure:(1) encoder module and (2) decoder module.

representation, and the decoder module recovers the original input from the compressed
code or encoded data for better representation. The first layer is of 800 hidden units and 200
hidden layer units for the second convolutional fusion layer.

deep learning model’s primary objective is to solve the learning problem in classical neural
networks training in deep layered networks, such as becoming stuck in local minima due to
poor parameter selection and slow learning massive amount of training datasets.

5 Conclusion and future direction

In this work, author proposed the automatic recognition system for cattle identification
using stacked denoised autoencoder and deep belief network method. The recognition

rection

In this work, author proposed the automatic recognition system for cattle identification
using stacked denoised autoencoder and deep belief network method. The recognition
framework uses the facial feature and minutiae features and fusion of multi-features at a
different level and yields 98.56% identification accuracy.
   The handcrafted feature descriptor methods are used to evaluate the identification accu-
racy. These methods are SIFT, Dense-SIFT, circular-LBP, SURF, and sparse representation
techniques yield better identification accuracy for cattle classification. The experimental
results on two biometric feature multi-modal are taken to measure the system accuracy
and illustrate the accurateness of the proposed system in comparison to existing advanced
recognition algorithms.
